repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: closest failed strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC span-lattice proposal; did not define the current active-set Riesz-Schur construction

published_prior_art.cite: DoudchenkoImbens2016, BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021, ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021, and AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 define component estimators, not a shared active-set certificate; sources include https://www.nber.org/papers/w22791, https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/01621459.2021.1929245, https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/aer.20190159, https://www.gsb.stanford.edu/faculty-research/publications/matrix-completion-methods-causal-panel-data-models

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: prior failed span-lattice kernel is closest, but this proposal adds the named Delta_{a,b,r} quotient certificate and staggered fixed-rank chart

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 and AtheyImbensQuViviano2025 discuss/merge MC, SC, unit weights, and time weights, but do not state an adjacent iff ATT-equality frontier; sources include https://www.gsb.stanford.edu/faculty-research/publications/matrix-completion-methods-causal-panel-data-models and https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.21536

ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 gives SDiD asymptotics and ChoiKwonLiao2024 gives low-rank-completion inference, but neither gives a sampling law for an adjacent-method equality diagnostic; sources include https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/aer.20190159 and https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0304407624000289

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: prior strictness witnesses were under-specified and single-shock; current claim asks for concrete noncorner staggered/rank witnesses

published_prior_art.cite: BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 and AbadieLHour2021 show imperfect-fit/penalty issues for SCM, not minimal noncorner witnesses for every SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC adjacent gap; source: https://economics.mit.edu/sites/default/files/publications/A%20Penalized%20Synthetic%20Control%20Estimator%20for%20Disagg.pdf

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison to AtheyImbensQuViviano2025/TROP is anchored only to 'abstract and estimator construction', not to a section, equation, page, or quoted gap for the specific ATT-equality frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The computability claim depends on displayed normal equations, but ASCM's outcome-model correction muhat and the SDiD objects X_T and X_D are not defined with enough dimensions/objectives to determine J_m, A_m, B_m, and hence Delta_{a,b,r}.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The SCM-ASCM concrete witness route uses weights (1/2+eta/2,1/2+eta/2), whose sum is 1+eta, so it violates the stated affine ASCM constraint one^T w=1 and is not yet a valid noncorner witness.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the exact ASCM ridge outcome-model objective and SDiD matrix dimensions before defining J_m, R_m, K_b, and Delta_{a,b,r}.
Replace the SCM-ASCM witness with weights satisfying one^T w=1, or explicitly define an intercept-augmented ASCM node if the intended affine correction allows common-shift absorption.
Add a section/equation/page anchor for TROP showing precisely where it combines low-rank structure with unit and time weights, then state why that object still lacks an equality-frontier certificate.

recommended_next_step: Revise the formal setup and the SCM-ASCM witness; the novelty kernel survives, but the certificate is not yet well-posed enough for acceptance.
